title: Marginaria arisanensis
taxonomy: Marginaria arisanensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae. It belongs to the genus Marginaria and is native to Taiwan. This plant is typically characterized by its succulent leaves and produces small white or yellow flowers. Marginaria arisanensis is a type of stonecrop, which is a group of plants known for their thick, fleshy leaves that store water. The exact classification of Marginaria arisanensis at the rank of order and family is within the Saxifragales order of plants.

intro: Marginaria arisanensis, also known as Arisan violet or Taiwanese violet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Taiwanese medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat. The bioactive constituents of Marginaria arisanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulations to promote respiratory health and immune function.
